metadata.title: Guideline on the clinical evaluation of medicinal products intended for treatment of hepatitis B

rules.rule_text: HBeAg negative chronic hepatitis B is established as a distinct disease entity from HBeAg positive chronic hepatitis B. Separate analyses are required, although a combined study with pre-planned analyses could be acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: The HBeAg negative chronic hepatitis B is established as a distinct disease entity that requires separate analyses although a combined study with pre-planned analyses could be considered acceptable.

rules.rule_text: Clinical trials for chronic hepatitis B must use PCR assays to establish baseline HBV DNA and monitor response during and after treatment. Validated and sensitive assays meeting current standards are essential.
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical trials of chronic hepatitis B require the use of PCR assays initially to establish a baseline DNA level and then continued use of PCR assays during antiviral therapy to measure accurately response and viral rebound associated with viral resistance. The use of validated and sensitive assays that meet current standards is essential.

rules.rule_text: The primary efficacy endpoint for non-interferon therapy of chronic hepatitis B in confirmatory trials should be a combined endpoint including virological, histological, and biochemical responses.
rules.verbatim_quote: the primary efficacy endpoint to antiviral therapy of chronic hepatitis B in confirmatory trials should, at present, especially for non-interferon therapy, be a combined endpoint including virological, histological and biochemical responses, and defined as a combined response.

rules.rule_text: Histological response is defined as decrease in HAI score by at least 2 points without worsening of fibrosis (by >=1 point). Any worsening of fibrosis should negate histological improvement in necroinflammatory scores. Assessment must be performed by a pathologist blinded to treatment allocation and biopsy timepoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: histological improvement is defined by decrease in HAI score by two points or more. This definition has not, however, been rigorously assessed or correlated with long-term clinical improvement... worsening of fibrosis (by = >1 point) is considered the most important intermediate endpoint in disease progression. Any worsening of fibrosis should negate histological improvement in necroinflammatory scores.

rules.rule_text: Patients in confirmatory trials must be well characterised as regards disease stage and type (HBeAg positive and negative), duration, activity and complications. Baseline data must include virological, serological, biochemical and histological parameters, with data on ALT, HBV DNA and HBeAg/anti-HBe on more than one occasion over a 6-month period.
rules.source_section: 2.2 Indications for therapy and inclusion criteria
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients included in confirmatory trials should be well characterised as regards disease stage and type (HBeAg positive and HBeAg negative), duration, activity and complications. Baseline data should include virological, serological, biochemical and histological parameters. Taking into account the fluctuating course of HBV infection, data on testing of ALT, HBV DNA and HBeAg/anti-HBe on more than one occasion over a 6-month period... should be included in the baseline assessment.

rules.rule_text: The documentation of disease severity with a well-established histological scoring system (grading necroinflammation and staging fibrosis) is always recommended. Absence of histology measurement must be carefully justified.
rules.source_section: 2.2 Indications for therapy and inclusion criteria
rules.verbatim_quote: The documentation of severity of disease with the aid of a well-established histological scoring system, including classification of grade of necroinflammatory activity and stage/extent of fibrosis, is always recommended. Absence of histology measurement must be carefully justified.

rules.rule_text: Study populations must include patients from different geographic regions, as well as men and women in sufficient numbers for meaningful subgroup analyses. HBV genotype (A-H) must be determined since it affects response to antiviral therapy.
rules.source_section: 2.2 Indications for therapy and inclusion criteria
rules.verbatim_quote: The selected study population should include patients from different geographic regions, as well as men and women in sufficient numbers to allow meaningful subgroup analyses... HBV genotype (A-H) needs to be determined, since recent data suggest that genotype may be related to clinical outcome and that genotype affects response to antiviral therapy.

rules.rule_text: A follow-up of 6-12 months post-treatment is necessary to define a sustained response. Criteria for a durable response should include long-term sustained virological and biochemical response and ultimately HBsAg seroconversion to anti-HBs.
rules.verbatim_quote: A follow-up of 6-12 months is necessary to define a sustained response to therapy. Criteria defining a durable response should include a long-term sustained virological and biochemical response and ultimately HBsAg seroconversion to anti-HBs.

rules.rule_text: For nucleos(t)ide analogues used as long-term therapy, response must be measured at multiple on-treatment timepoints using a hierarchical system of initial, end-of-treatment, and maintained response. Liver biopsies at defined intervals are recommended to confirm maintained response.
rules.verbatim_quote: With respect to nucleos(t)ide analogues, the need for clearer definitions of timing of responses has become evident. The response to antiviral therapy should be measured on several occasions both on-treatment, at end-of-treatment and after cessation of treatment and characterised in a hierarchical system of chronological endpoints: initial, end-off-treatment and sustained response... During long-term therapy (i.e. more than a year), as applicable for nucleos(t)ide analogues, liver biopsies in a subset of patients at defined intervals is recommended to confirm a maintained response.

rules.rule_text: For approval of new antiviral agents for chronic hepatitis B, optimal treatment duration and timepoint for cessation must be at least provisionally defined. Clear criteria for stopping therapy must be laid down.
rules.verbatim_quote: For approval of new antiviral agents in the treatment of chronic hepatitis B, optimal treatment duration and timepoint that allows cessation of therapy need to have been at least provisionally defined. Clear criteria for stopping therapy must be laid down

rules.rule_text: The mode of action and potential mechanisms of viral resistance must be well described in pre-clinical studies. Any potential for virus already resistant to other agents to show cross-resistance to the new agent must be explored in detail.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that the antiviral activity and mechanism(s) of action of the novel agent have been well characterised in vitro... any potential for virus that is already resistant to other agent(s) to show cross-resistance to the antiviral agent under evaluation should be explored in detail.

rules.rule_text: Investigation of genotypic and phenotypic resistance and resistance mechanisms is an essential element of drug development. The post-licensing programme should aim to identify resistance-associated mutations and appropriate breakpoints for in-vitro susceptibility test results.
rules.verbatim_quote: Investigation of genotypic and phenotypic resistance and resistance mechanisms is an essential element of drug development. The choice of assays and assay conditions should be justified. The post-licensing development programme should aim to identify resistance-associated mutations and appropriate breakpoints to be applied to in vitro susceptibility test results.

rules.rule_text: Each indication for chronic hepatitis B should ideally be supported by at least one randomised and double-blind trial. All personnel involved in assessing histological or laboratory results should be blinded to treatment assignment.
rules.verbatim_quote: Ideally, each indication for the treatment of chronic hepatitis B that is sought should be supported by at least one randomised and double blind trial. Where a combined primary efficacy variable has been defined that includes results of histological studies or of laboratory tests, all the personnel involved should be blinded as to the assigned treatment.

rules.rule_text: Long-term clinical trials of at least one year are needed to exclude clinically relevant inferiority for compounds assumed to be equally effective. Study design must include a 6-12 month post-treatment period to allow assessment of sustained response and withdrawal effects.
rules.verbatim_quote: At least one year (if the compound is pre-planned for long-term treatment) is needed to exclude clinically relevant inferiority for compounds assumed to be equally effective... The study design and duration should allow adequate assessment of a sustained response and potential effects of withdrawal of treatment i.e. include a 6-12 month period post treatment.

rules.rule_text: Monitoring of clinical resistance must take place at baseline, on-treatment, and at week 48 (and at pre-defined intervals if long-term therapy). Partial sequencing of isolates should be performed on increases in viral load to predefined levels (e.g. >1 log). A pre-specified plan for action in the event of resistance emergence must be in place.
rules.verbatim_quote: Monitoring of clinical resistance should take place at baseline, on-treatment and at week 48 (and at pre-defined intervals if long-term therapy is planned) and partial sequencing of isolates should be performed on increases in viral load to predefined levels (e.g. >1 log). If resistance emerges, a pre-specified plan for action and monitoring of these patients should be in place.

rules.rule_text: The active comparator must be among the best or optimal available therapies. Clinical trials aimed at supporting a first-line indication must always include comparison with the accepted first-line treatment.
rules.source_section: 4.3.2 Choice of comparator in randomised controlled trials
rules.verbatim_quote: The active comparative regimen that is chosen for any one trial must be considered to be the best therapy, or among the optimal available therapies, for the condition being treated in all patients eligible for study... clinical trials aimed at supporting a first line indication should always include comparison with the accepted first line treatment.

rules.rule_text: For combination therapy studies, clinical trials should compare the combination with both the novel and the second agent alone. Continuing studies of viral dynamics using quantitative PCR and emergence of (multi)resistant variants are required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Whenever circumstances permit, clinical studies should compare the combination with both the novel and the second agent alone. Such a study design not only allows for an assessment of any additive and synergistic effect of combining the two drugs, but also facilitates the interpretation of the safety data. Continuing studies of viral dynamics using the quantitative PCR techniques and emergence of (multi)resistant variants will be required

rules.rule_text: Separate efficacy trials in children are considered necessary due to higher rates of spontaneous remission in paediatric chronic HBV. An active versus placebo design should be pursued when ethically justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Considering the different disease characteristics in children and adults, in particular the higher rate of spontaneous remission of chronic HBV disease in children, separate efficacy trials in children are considered necessary. When an active versus placebo design is possible and ethically justified, it should be pursued.

rules.rule_text: For decompensated cirrhotic/liver transplant patients, placebo-controlled trials are not ethical. An active control comparator should be used. On-therapy monitoring of clinical status and viral load must be performed every 3 months with dose adjustments based on creatinine clearance.
rules.source_section: 5.2 Decompensated Cirrhotic Patients including Liver Transplant Patients
rules.verbatim_quote: Placebo controlled trials are not considered ethical in this severely ill patient population and an active control comparator medication should be used if available... on-therapy monitoring of clinical status and viral load must be performed at defined intervals (e.g. every 3 months) and individualised depending on the risk of decompensation in the event of emergence of resistance.

rules.rule_text: For HIV/HBV co-infected patients, treatment of HBV should not impact negatively on antiretroviral therapy. Monotherapy studies with agents active against HIV cannot be performed in ART-naive HIV-infected cases at doses active against HIV.
rules.source_section: 5.3 Co-infected patients with HBV and HIV
rules.verbatim_quote: Specific considerations in the choice of study therapy must be taken in patients, who are co-infected with HBV and HIV, since treatment of HBV infection should not impact negatively on antiretroviral therapy (ART). Monotherapy studies with such agents in ART-naive HIV-infected cases cannot be performed at doses active against HIV

rules.rule_text: Special attention must be given to withdrawal effects after drug discontinuation, particularly the risk of symptomatic/asymptomatic hepatitis flares and hepatic decompensation, which should be carefully evaluated for several months after discontinuation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special attention should be given to withdrawal effects after drug discontinuation, in particular the risk of development of symptomatic/asymptomatic hepatitis flares and hepatic decompensation which should be carefully evaluated for several months after discontinuation of hepatitis B therapy.

rules.rule_text: In combination trials, the combination must be compared to each drug alone during the clinical development programme to exclude an increased risk of toxicity.
rules.verbatim_quote: In combination trials, the combination should be compared to two drugs alone during the clinical development programme, since an increased risk of toxicity must be excluded.
